channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Suvendu Adhikari to be sworn in as first BJP chief minister of West Bengal
item.description: Suvendu Adhikari is all set to assume office as the first Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) chief minister of West Bengal on May 9, 2026, following a landmark victory in the state assembly elections. The o...

item.title: Driver’s license suspended following Quezon City gas-and-run case
item.description: The Land Transportation Office (LTO) has officially suspended the license of a driver involved in a gas-and-run incident in Quezon City. The incident took place on May 3, 2026, at 3:16 a.m. at a UniOi...

item.title: Kenya Ministry of Health Issues Advisory Amid Cruise Ship Hantavirus Cases
item.description: Kenya's Ministry of Health has issued a public health advisory after a hantavirus outbreak was reported aboard the cruise ship MV Hondius in the Atlantic Ocean. While no cases have been recorded in Ke...

item.title: Drake Maye Supports Coach Mike Vrabel Amid Off-Field Controversy
item.description: New England Patriots quarterback Drake Maye has publicly expressed his support for head coach Mike Vrabel despite the coach being involved in a widely reported off-field scandal. During an appearance ...

item.title: Teaser Released for Spanish Sci-Fi Film 'Pacifico' at Cannes Market
item.description: The Spanish-language science fiction film 'Pacifico' has released its first teaser ahead of the Cannes Market 2026. Directed by Gonzalo Gutierrez and produced by Mauricio Brunetti and Guido Rud of Fil...

item.title: No Jackpot Winners for Ultra Lotto 6/58 and Megalotto 6/45 on May 8, 2026
item.description: On Friday, May 8, 2026, the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) reported that there were no winners for the major lottery draws held that day. For the Ultra Lotto 6/58, the winning combinatio...

item.title: Prosecution Witness Explains Documents and Transactions of Property Linked to Yahaya Bello
item.description: For FCT High Court, Abuja, one prosecution witness, Ramalan Abdullahi wey dey work as lawyer for Federal Capital Authority, yan how him prepare documents for property wey dey linked to former Kogi Sta...

item.title: ISIS brides face court over alleged slavery and terror offences; Farrer by-election political outlook
item.description: In the latest episode of The Nightly, reporter Ben O’Shea provides an overview of significant developments in Australian politics and justice. Three women, referred to as 'ISIS brides', have appeared ...

item.title: Smart Applications Introduces AI to Improve Healthcare Claims Accuracy and Reduce Fraud
item.description: Smart Applications International, a tech firm operating across 11 African countries, has launched Smart Detect AI, an artificial intelligence platform aimed at tackling fraud, waste, and abuse in heal...

item.title: Devendra Fadnavis claims AIMIM leader was involved in concealing Nida Khan in TCS conversion matter
item.description: In a recent statement regarding the TCS conversion case, Maharashtra Deputy Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis alleged that a leader from AIMIM had a role in hiding Nida Khan. The case has been under pu...

item.title: Final report on Air India AI-171 crash expected within a month as probe nears completion
item.description: The investigation into the tragic crash of Air India Flight AI-171, which occurred in Ahmedabad in June 2025 and claimed 260 lives, is now in its final stage, according to Union Civil Aviation Ministe...

item.title: Laikipia Residents See Light as Rumuruti-Mutara Road Upgrade Begins
item.description: For decades, residents of Mutara in Laikipia County have suffered from impassable roads, causing delays in transport, post-harvest losses, and limited access to hospitals. Vehicles frequently got stuc...

item.title: Canada Monitors Impact of AI Data Centres on Electricity Grid
item.description: Canada is actively observing a recent alert issued by the North American Electric Reliability Corporation (NERC), highlighting increasing pressures on electricity grids across North America. The warni...

item.title: Carlton shows fight despite 1-8 start, with strong second-half performance against Brisbane
item.description: Carlton’s AFL season may be off to a rough start with a 1-8 record, but their performance against reigning premiers Brisbane Lions offers a glimpse of optimism. Despite falling 49 points behind early ...

item.title: Federal High Court Upholds $100 Million Award Against CNPC in OPL 471 Dispute
item.description: For Port Harcourt, the Federal High Court don reject China National Petroleum Corporation (CNPC) attempt to overturn $100 million judgement wey Cutra International Limited collect over ownership wahal...

item.title: Nicholas Kaufman Steps Down from Representing Duterte at ICC
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – Nicholas Kaufman, the lawyer who represented former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte in his International Criminal Court (ICC) case, has formally requested to withdraw as Dut...

item.title: Tanzanian Stars Nandy and Abigail Chams to Perform at Africa Forward Summit in Nairobi
item.description: Tanzania inafika kubwa kwenye Africa Forward Summit 2026 kwa sababu ya Nandy na Abigail Chams watakaonyesha talent yao huko Nairobi. Wote wawili wameratibiwa kuonekana kwenye Africa Forward: Le Concer...

item.title: Green Party Wins Control of Norwich City Council, Marking Key Local Election Victory
item.description: In a significant development in this year’s local elections, the Green Party has won control of Norwich City Council, taking over from the Labour Party. This represents the party’s first council victo...

item.title: Nicholas Kaufman formally requests to leave Duterte's defense team at ICC
item.description: British-Israeli lawyer Nicholas Kaufman has formally requested the International Criminal Court (ICC) to allow him to withdraw from representing former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte. Kaufman ci...

item.title: APC Urges Nigerians to Give Tinubu Time to Improve Economy
item.description: The National Publicity Secretary of the All Progressives Congress (APC), Felix Morka, don talk say President Bola Tinubu still need more time to fulfil im promises for Nigeria. For TVC News’ This Morn...
